how_computed: Percentage of gold test cases (one per numbered step across every enabled agent spec in the build manifest) that the agent's emitted registry reproduces under a matching tc_id of the form [agent]-step-[step_id]. A gold case with no matching emitted tc_id counts as a gap. Denominator = total steps extracted from all enabled agent specs by the deterministic reference (agents/common/testcase_spec.py).
tiebreaker_how_computed: Over the gold cases the agent DID reproduce, the percentage of the eleven registry fields (tc_id, agent, step_id, step_ext, the five involves_* booleans, expected_outcome, fail_condition) that exactly equal the deterministic gold field. Booleans match exactly; string fields match verbatim. A field the agent omitted counts as a mismatch.
rationale: Every framework receives the same build manifest and the same agent-node spec cards and must reproduce the exact per-step test-case registry: extracting every numbered step (including sub-lettered steps like 3b), forming the [agent]-step-[step_id] tc_id, copying step_ext verbatim, and computing the five case-sensitive involves_* substring flags, the capital-'Assert ' expected_outcome clauses, and the 'Fail:' fail_condition off the Metric line. The fixtures deliberately span HTTP/DB/file-write/assert/metric flag triggers, sub-lettered steps, an Assert-clause step, a '÷'/'rate' metric step, a PARSE_ERROR node with no How section, and a disabled node that must be excluded, so a framework that fumbles any rule loses cases or cells. The deterministic n600 deliverable (results/test-case-registry.json) is gap-free by construction and is what n601 (Postman Collection Creator) consumes; this metric ranks which framework most reliably reproduces it.
fail_condition: Fail: any step from any enabled agent spec that is absent from the registry — gap_count > 0 makes the registry incomplete; a registry with gaps_found = true must not be consumed by n601 until the gap is resolved and n600 is re-run to 100%.
